tex:
Navja works in a post office.

(a) The table shows the costs of sending parcels by post.
The cost depends on the mass, $m$ grams, of the parcel.

\begin{tabular}{|l|c|c|}
\hline
Type of parcel & Mass (g) & Cost (\$) \\
\hline
Small & $0 < m \leqslant 60$ & 0.76 \\
Medium & $60 < m \leqslant 100$ & 0.95 \\
Large & $100 < m \leqslant 250$ & 2.20 \\
Extra large & $250 < m \leqslant 1000$ & 5.60 \\
\hline
\end{tabular}
(i) (b) Sai sends each of these four parcels by post.

% Four parcels: 55g, 0.3kg, 875g, 250g

He pays with a \$20 note.

Work out how much change he receives.

(c) On 1 April, the cost of sending any parcel increases by 5\%.

\begin{subsubparts}

(d) Show that the increase in the cost of sending an **Extra large** parcel is \$0.28.

(e) Avani says

``As the cost of an **Extra large** parcel increases by \$0.28 then the cost of a **Large** parcel will also increase by \$0.28 to \$2.48.''

Explain why Avani is incorrect.

\end{subsubparts}

(f) (i) (g) Navja weighs a parcel with mass $w$\,kg on her scales.
She uses the masses shown to balance the scales.

% Diagram of scales with w kg + 5g + 10g + 20g on one side, 2kg on the other

Work out the value of $w$.

$w = $ 

(h) Sometimes Navja uses an electronic weighing machine.
The machine gives the mass, $p$\,kg, of a parcel as 12.4\,kg, correct to the nearest 100\,g.

Complete this statement about the value of $p$.

 $\leqslant p <$

Medium$60 < m \leqslant 100$0.95
Large$100 < m \leqslant 250$2.20
Extra large$250 < m \leqslant 1000$5.60
(i) (b) Sai sends each of these four parcels by post. % Four parcels: 55g, 0.3kg, 875g, 250g He pays with a \$20 note. Work out how much change he receives. (c) On 1 April, the cost of sending any parcel increases by 5\%. \begin{subsubparts} (d) Show that the increase in the cost of sending an **Extra large** parcel is \$0.28. (e) Avani says ``As the cost of an **Extra large** parcel increases by \$0.28 then the cost of a **Large** parcel will also increase by \$0.28 to \$2.48.'' tex:
(a) 66 football players each take five penalties.
The number of penalties that each player scores is recorded.
The results are shown in the bar chart.

% Bar chart showing:
% 0 penalties: 2
% 1 penalty: 19
% 2 penalties: 14
% 3 penalties: 10
% 4 penalties: 5
% 5 penalties: 16 (approximately, based on visual)

(i) (b) Write down the mode.

(c) Write down the range.

(d) Calculate the mean.

(e) The attendance at a football match is 11\,678.

(i) (f) Write 11\,678 in words.

(g) Write 11\,678 correct to the nearest 100.

(h) In a football stadium there are 15\,000 seats.
10\,650 of these seats are occupied.

Find the percentage of the 15\,000 seats that are occupied.

(i) A ticket to a football match costs \$20.

Calculate the cost of the ticket in rupees when the exchange rate is 1 rupee $= \$0.016$.

(a) % Diagram of front of house: rectangle 9m wide, 5m high, with triangular roof 2.4m high
% 4 windows and 1 door shown

The diagram shows the front of Pranav's house.

(i) (b) Work out the total area of the front of his house.

(c) The door is 0.9\,m wide and 2.1\,m high.
Each of the four windows are 1.5\,m wide and 1.2\,m high.

Work out the total area of the door and the four windows.

(d) Pranav paints the front of his house but not the door and not the four windows.

Work out the area he paints.

(e) Pranav paints a wall of area 53\,m$^2$.
One litre of paint covers an area of 4.5\,m$^2$.
Paint is sold in 2.5 litre tins, each costing \$24.75.
Pranav buys the least number of tins to paint this wall.

Work out the cost of the paint.

On the grid, draw the image of

(c) shape $B$ after a reflection in the line $y = -3$.

The scale is 1 centimetre represents 6 metres.

(c) A flagpole, $F$, is on a bearing of $164°$ from $S$.

Work out the bearing of $S$ from $F$.

(d) Ishaan uses the map to find some treasure, $T$.
$T$ is on a bearing of $076°$ from $R$ and on a bearing of $337°$ from $S$.

Mark the position of $T$ on the map.

tex:
The grid shows the first three diagrams in a sequence.
Each diagram is made using small squares that are white or grey.

% Diagram 1: 2×2 grid (1 white in center, 3 grey on border)
% Diagram 2: 3×3 grid (4 white, 5 grey)
% Diagram 3: 4×4 grid (9 white, 7 grey)
% Diagram 4: to be drawn by student

(a) On the grid, draw Diagram 4.

(b) Write down the term to term rule for the number of grey squares.

(c) \begin{tabular}{|l|c|c|c|c|c|}
\hline
Diagram number & 1 & 2 & 3 & 4 & $n$ \\
\hline
Number of small white squares & 1 & 4 & 9 & & \\
\hline
Number of small grey squares & 3 & 5 & 7 & & \\
\hline
Total number of small squares & 4 & 9 & 16 & & \\
\hline
\end{tabular}
Complete the table.

(d) Work out the number of small white squares in Diagram 18.

(e) One of the diagrams has a total of 900 small squares.

Work out its Diagram number.

Diagram 

(f) Another diagram has 43 small grey squares.

Work out the total number of small squares in this diagram.
